name: ghost-align
description: Visually align operators like = without modifying your source code

ghostAlign.showStatusBar.description: Show a status bar item indicating Ghost Align ON/OFF (click to toggle)

ghostAlign.operators.description: Operators to align (used when the current language is not listed in ghostAlign.operatorsByLanguage). Supported special tokens: "=" (assignment), ":" (JSON/YAML key or CSS declaration), "//" and "#" (trailing line comments). Any other string is matched literally. The list order is the priority: the first operator found on a line is the one aligned, so only one column is aligned per line.

ghostAlign.operatorsByLanguage.description: Per-language operator overrides keyed by languageId (e.g. "json", "jsonc", "typescript"). If the current document's language is listed here, this list is used in place of ghostAlign.operators. Each value is a list of operators (see ghostAlign.operators for supported tokens such as "=", ":", "//", "#"); e.g. set "typescript": ["//"] to align trailing comments.

ghostAlign.ghostCharacter.description: Character used for ghost padding. Defaults to a non-breaking space (U+00A0), which renders like a half-width space but is not collapsed by the editor's rendering. Plain ASCII spaces will be collapsed to one cell and break alignment — use NBSP or en-space (U+2002) if you customize this. Leaving this empty in the settings UI falls back to the default.

ghostAlign.ghostColor.description: Tint color of the ghost padding (any CSS color string, e.g. "rgba(128,128,128,0.25)", "#88888840", "transparent"). Applied as both foreground and background so whitespace padding is distinguishable from real spaces. Leaving this empty in the settings UI falls back to the default; set "transparent" to disable tinting entirely.

scripts.package:install: npm run package && npm run install:vsix
